title: gumira punduana
intro: Gumira punduana, also known as Punduana, is a plant species native to South America, particularly in the Andean region, that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of Gumira punduana include infusions, decoctions, and infusions in combination with other plants, which are typically consumed as teas or taken in capsule form.
